content.problemStatement:
You are given two sorted arrays AAA and BBB of size NNN and MMM respectively. You need to merge these two arrays and keep the final array sorted.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers NNN and MMM — the size of array AAA and BBB
The second line contains all the elements of array AAA
The third line contains all the elements of array BBB


Output Format
Output the merged array elements on a single line.
constraints: Sum of (N+M) over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines: the first line contains two integers N and M — the sizes of the sorted arrays A and B. The second line contains N integers in non‑decreasing order — the elements of A. The third line contains M integers in non‑decreasing order — the elements of B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N+M integers — the merged array in non‑decreasing order, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: A = [1,4,8,9,10], B = [2,3,5,6]; merging keeps the overall order resulting in [1,2,3,4,5,6,8,9,10].
A = [10], B = [1,2]; after merging we obtain [1,2,10].
Interleaving the two already sorted arrays yields a fully sorted sequence.

solution.approach:
Both input arrays are already sorted. Using the two‑pointer technique we can build the merged array in linear time: keep an index for each array, repeatedly output the smaller current element and advance its pointer. When one array is exhausted, append the remaining elements of the other array. This yields a correctly sorted merged sequence without extra sorting.
The algorithm runs in O(N+M) per test case and uses O(1) additional memory besides the output buffer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N+M) per test case, O(total N+M) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N+M) for the output, O(1) extra auxiliary space
